metadata.category: Arrays | Sorting | Binary Search | Two Pointers

content.problemStatement:
Zonal Computing Olympiad 2015, 29 Nov 2014
We say that two integers x and y have a variation of at least K, if |x − y| ≥ K (the absolute value of their difference is at least K). Given a sequence of N integers a1,a2,...,aN and K, the total variation count is the number of pairs of elements in the sequence with variation at least K, i.e. it is the size of the set of pairs


 {(i,j)|1 ≤ i < j ≤ N and|ai−aj|≥K} 


For example if K = 1 and the sequence is 3,2,4 the answer is 3. If K = 1 and the sequence is 3, 1, 3 then the answer is 2.


Your task is to write a program that takes a sequence and the value K as input and computes the total variation count.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers N and K (1 ≤ N ≤ 65000, 1 ≤ K ≤ 10^8). The second line contains N integers a1 … aN (0 ≤ ai ≤ 10^8) separated by spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total variation count – the number of pairs (i, j) with i < j and |ai‑aj| ≥ K.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: pairs (3,1) and (1,3) satisfy |x‑y|≥1 → 2. Second: only pair (3,1) satisfies ≥2 → 1. Third: all 10 possible pairs have difference at least 1.
Sorted array is [1,4,7,10]. For each element, the number of later elements with difference ≥3 are: 3,2,1,0 → total 6.
K=0 counts every pair. With 5 elements there are 5·4/2 = 10 pairs.

solution.approach: For each test case sort the array. For every index i (0‑based) we need the smallest index j > i such that a[j] - a[i] ≥ K. Because the array is sorted, a[j] - a[i] is non‑decreasing with j, so we can find j with binary search (or a moving pointer). All elements from j to N‑1 form valid pairs with i, contributing N‑j pairs. Summing this over all i yields the answer. The algorithm runs in O(N log N) time per test case and O(N) extra memory for the sorted array. Use 64‑bit integer for the answer because the number of pairs can be up to N·(N‑1)/2.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case (sorting dominates).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the array.
